texto: It could be the sound of each name he knows/curling to ash in his chest’s aortic furnace one after another, year after year instructing him/in the patient work of letting go. Even still/there are things it is reluctant to unclasp./How the Osage orange trunks and bare limbs/glow in the scattered light like veins of fire.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Bryan Penberthy: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “It could be the sound of each name he knows/curling to ash in his chest’s aortic furnace one afte…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, grief, letting-go pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e trabalho.
